files.Title: KSP.Sim.impl.CollisionManager
files.Summary:
Manage PhysX interactions to ignore collisions between colliders in the same vessel.

The primary mechanism for this is Physics.IgnoreCollision(). However, doing that for every collider combination scales O(n^2). Scalability problems become measurable around 100 parts, and can take several minutes after 900+ parts.

It is much faster to only set up ignores for parts that are actually touching. This scales close to O(n) with the collider count. However, it's difficult to test this in advance. So we just let PhysX tell us which are touching, because that's what it's designed to do.

  • Record each collider as being "owned" by a vessel (guid)
  • Wait for PhysX to report a contact.
  • CollisionMonitor receives a Physics.ContactModifyEvent for the contact. (The callback is offthread)
  • If the contact is between to colliders owned by the same vessel GUID, void the contact. (This allows them to pass through as if already ignored)
  • Log the contact in a thread-safe structure.
  • At some later point on the main thread, read the log, set do the Physics.IgnoreCollision(), and (optionally) log again so that it can be undone.

There are some cases that require special handling:

  • Collider setups that won't generate Physics.ContactModifyEvent() callbacks (usually because they are temporarily on the same Rigidbody), but need to be ignored anyway. It is necessary to call Physics.IgnoreCollision() in advance. ("always ignore")
  • Some ignores need to persist despite the collider being removed from the vessel. ("persist after removal"). This is usually a separable item (e.g., engine shroud) significantly overlaps other colliders (e.g., the engine it contains). If the ignore is removed too soon, the resulting contact event can lead to RUD.

Listen for physics contacts from Physics.ContactModifyEvent(), void contacts between the same vessel, and report them to the owning CollisionManager when that happens.

This process is set up as a singleton separate from CollisionManager in order to avoid multiple CollisionManagers (per vessel) getting callbacks for colliders they don't own.

files.Title: Redux.Fixes.DresRingsFix
files.Summary: The rings are displayed behind Dres because of some depth test issues between the local space planet shader and the scaled space rings. This fixes the issue by changing the local-scaled transition to be below the inner radius of the rings (250km to be low enough below the ring's inner radius is 414km).

files.Title: DocumentLocalization
files.Summary:
A component which automatically localizes all elements in a document. Only elements with a string property "text" whose value is a localization key starting with '#' will be localized.

The LocalizationManager.OnLocalizeEvent is handled to automatically update the localization of all registered elements when the game language is changed.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Bindings.CommandBinding
files.Summary:
Convenience binding for Button.clicked -> ICommand. Wires the button's clicked event and enable state to a parameterless command on the data source.

UXML usage:

<ui:Button name="buy-btn" text="Buy">
    <Bindings>
        <CommandBinding property="text" data-source-path="BuyCommand" />
    </Bindings>
</ui:Button>

The "property" attribute is required by the infrastructure but unused - set it to any valid property (by convention "name"). For non-Button elements, use EventBinding.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Bindings.EventBinding
files.Summary:
Binds any UI Toolkit event on any VisualElement to an ICommand or ICommand<TEvent> on the data source. Works with every EventBase subclass - clicks, pointer, keyboard, focus, change, drag, navigation, and more.

UXML usage:

<!-- Parameterless: event fires command, data discarded -->
<EventBinding property="name" data-source-path="HoverCommand" event-type="PointerEnterEvent" />

<!-- Typed: ViewModel receives full event object -->
<EventBinding property="value" data-source-path="KeyDownCommand" event-type="KeyDownEvent" />

<!-- ChangeEvent special case: extracts newValue automatically -->
<EventBinding property="value" data-source-path="VolumeCommand" event-type="ChangeEvent<System.Single>" />

The "property" attribute is required by the infrastructure but unused - set it to any valid property (e.g. "name"). Reflection runs only once per command instance - subsequent updates only sync enabled state.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Bindings.EventTypeResolver
files.Summary: Resolves event type name strings from UXML attributes to System.Type objects. Supports simple names ("KeyDownEvent") and generic names ("ChangeEvent<System.Single>"). Results are cached so reflection runs only once per unique type string.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Commands.ICommand<T>
files.Summary: Generic command that receives a parameter of type T. T can be a UI event type (e.g. KeyDownEvent) or a value type extracted from ChangeEvent<T> by EventBinding.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Commands.ICommand
files.Summary: Parameterless command interface. Use for actions where no event data is needed: button clicks, pointer enter/leave, focus/blur, submit, etc.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Commands.RelayCommand<T>
files.Summary: Concrete parameterized command backed by delegates. T is typically a UI event type or a value extracted from ChangeEvent<T>.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Commands.RelayCommand
files.Summary: Concrete parameterless command backed by delegates.

files.Title: UitkForKsp2.MVVM.Core.ViewModelBase
